meta.status_reason: Edge score 79 lands in the 60-80 provisional band: trigger clarity is high (scheduled prints), but the variant departure is moderate and partially covered by one contrarian house.
meta.next_action: Surface candidates; monitor falsifiers; consider handoff of the top expression once its data pool is populated.

headline: RBI's Monetary Policy Committee cuts repo rate 50 bps to 5.00% in surprise off-cycle move

M0_1.event_statement: The Reserve Bank of India's Monetary Policy Committee lowered the policy repo rate by 50 basis points to 5.00% on 10 June 2026, in an off-cycle decision passed by a 5-1 vote. The committee recorded May headline CPI of 2.9% and kept the policy stance at neutral. The decision was published in an RBI press release the same morning.
M0_1.source_grade_rationale: Official policy-authority publication; Reuters wire corroborates.
M0_1.causal_language_review: Checked and removed: 'surprise' (headline adjective), 'citing' (causal attribution) - replaced with 'recorded'. No because/due to/driven by present in the saved statement.
M0_1.60_second_check_note: RBI press release opened and rate/vote/stance verified at 2026-06-10T05:20Z.

world_changes.change_description: 10-year G-sec yield fell on the announcement day
world_changes.source: Exchange data (NSE/CCIL close), Reuters market wrap

world_changes.change_description: Overnight call money rate repriced lower

M0_2.gate_rationale: Three already-occurred, quantified changes against stated baselines (2-6 required).
watchlist_deferred_items: Bank MCLR/EBLR resets (will occur on reset dates - not yet occurred)
Housing-finance company funding-cost decline (expected over 1-2 quarters - not yet occurred)

direct_beneficiaries.industry_category: Housing finance companies (retail mortgage lenders funded largely by market borrowings)
direct_beneficiaries.mechanism: Funding cost is repo-linked via CPs/NCDs and bank lines (WC-001, WC-003); mortgage book yields reprice slower, expanding spread.

scoring.scoring_notes: 1-step funding-cost link (25); magnitude estimated from 50 bps x borrowing mix (15); benefit lands over 1-2 quarters as borrowings roll (15); a policy reversal inside the horizon is unlikely after an off-cycle cut (25).

direct_beneficiaries.mechanism: Same repo-linked funding-cost decline (WC-001, WC-003) on shorter-duration books.

scoring.scoring_notes: Shorter books reprice both sides faster - spread benefit is real but partially competed away (reversibility 15).

indirect_beneficiaries.gics_classification: Real Estate / Real Estate Management & Development (GICS 60201030)
indirect_beneficiaries.two_step_mechanism: Cheaper mortgages (via DIR-001 repricing) lift housing demand, then presales (WC-001 two steps removed).

scoring.scoring_notes: 2-step (15); demand elasticity directional only (5); lagged beyond the horizon (5).

harmed_parties.industry_category: Banks with high repo-linked (EBLR) floating retail books and sticky deposit costs
harmed_parties.mechanism: EBLR loans reprice down within a quarter (WC-001); term-deposit costs reset slower, compressing NIM 1-2 quarters.

scoring.scoring_notes: Mechanical repricing (25); magnitude estimated from EBLR share disclosures (15); hits the very next reporting quarter (25); reverses as deposits reprice (15).

M0_3.pair_trade_notes: Natural pair: long housing-finance (DIR-001, funding-led spread expansion) vs short high-EBLR-share banking (HARM-001, NIM compression) - isolates the rate-transmission asymmetry from market beta.

M0_4.horizon_rationale: Funding-cost transmission to HFC/NBFC P&L runs over 1-2 reporting quarters as borrowings roll; the Q2 FY27 results window (Oct-Nov 2026) is the visibility point.
M0_4.expiry_condition: Publication of Q2 FY27 (Sep-quarter) results for the major listed housing-finance companies (Oct-Nov 2026 results season).
M0_4.horizon_expiry_market_signal: 3-month CP rates for AAA HFCs renormalizing toward the new repo level (CCIL/exchange data).

M0_5.falsification_sentence: If 3-month AAA HFC commercial-paper yields have not declined by at least 25 bps from their 2026-06-09 level by 2026-08-15, the funding-cost transmission this thesis depends on is not occurring.
M0_5.monitorable_metric_1: 3-month AAA-rated HFC CP yield (CCIL / exchange money-market data)
M0_5.monitorable_metric_2: 1-year AAA NBFC NCD yield (exchange bond data)
M0_5.uncomfortable_check_rationale: If short funding does not reprice, the entire DIR-001 spread-expansion mechanism is dead - there is no fallback story.

secondary_falsifiers.description: MPC reverses the cut (hike) at or before the August 2026 meeting
Deposit-rate war keeps HFC bank-line costs flat despite the repo cut
secondary_falsifiers.monitorable_metric: WALR on fresh rupee loans (RBI monthly data)

consensus_view.paragraph: The street reads the off-cycle 50 bps cut as confirmation that the easing cycle is front-loaded and nearly done: with CPI at 2.9% and real rates still positive, most houses pencil one final 25 bps cut at most, then a long hold. Rate-sensitive financials rallied broadly on the day, and the consensus trade is simply 'own quality lenders into a stable-rate environment'; the transmission detail is treated as already in the price after the day-one re-rating.
consensus_view.numeric_anchor: Median street terminal repo forecast 4.75-5.00%; NIFTY Financial Services +2.1% on 2026-06-10.
key_consensus_assumptions: Transmission to lender funding costs is immediate and already priced after the day-one rally.
HFC spread benefit is offset by competitive pass-through to borrowers within one quarter.
Bank NIM compression from EBLR repricing is small (<10 bps) and recovers within two quarters.

contrary_view.paragraph: One macro-strategy house argues short-funding renormalization will lag because system liquidity is only at neutral, muting the HFC benefit this cycle.
contrary_view.contrary_numeric_anchor: Their CP-repo spread forecast: +35 bps persistent vs +15 bps historical.
contrary_view_sources: Named strategist note summarized in Moneycontrol, 2026-06-10 (labelled: secondary aggregator, grade B)

consensus_strength.unanimity_rationale: Clear majority on 'easing nearly done, own lenders', with one documented dissent on transmission speed.

analyst_rating_distribution.missing_reason: No single-issuer subject at this stage (industry-level thesis); rating distributions deferred to candidate-level work.

estimate_dispersion.missing_reason: Industry-level subject; estimate dispersion meaningful only per issuer - searched aggregate dispersion data 2026-06-10, none published at sector level.

M0_6_1.entrenchment_note: Index flows into financials reinforce the simple 'own lenders' consensus regardless of transmission detail.

data.note: NIFTY Financial Services consensus NTM EPS: +0.4% revision over 3m, +0.9% over 1m (aggregator data, 2026-06-10, labelled).

estimate_revision_trajectory.interpretation: Mildly positive drift; no step-change yet attributable to the cut.

data.note: NIFTY Financial Services fwd P/E 16.8x vs 5-year band 13.5-17.5x (aggregator, 2026-06-10): pricing a benign, near-upper-band scenario.

implied_scenario_from_multiple.interpretation: The sector multiple already sits in the top third of its range - the easy re-rating happened on day one.

options_implied_move.missing_reason: No single-issuer subject; index option IV not probative for the HFC-specific mechanism. Searched NSE option chain 2026-06-10.

short_interest_positioning.missing_reason: India lacks a comparable short-interest print; futures OI shifts deferred to candidate level.

M0_6_2.implied_scenario_interpretation: Blocks 2 and 3 together say the market is pricing 'good news, fully transmitted': a near-top-of-band sector multiple with only mild estimate drift. Nothing in the priced scenario distinguishes funding-mix winners (market-borrowing-funded HFCs) from deposit-funded laggards - the cross-sectional detail the thesis lives on.

M0_6_3.variant_paragraph: The market re-rated the whole financials complex as if a 50 bps cut benefits all lenders equally. It does not: lenders funded by market borrowings (CP/NCD) capture the repricing within one to two quarters while asset yields lag, expanding spreads; deposit-funded banks with high EBLR shares see the OPPOSITE - assets reprice first. The cross-sectional spread between those two funding models is not in estimates yet.
M0_6_3.consensus_numeric_view: Sector-level: ~+1% NTM EPS drift, uniform across lenders (M0.6.2 block 2).
M0_6_3.our_numeric_view: Market-borrowing-funded HFCs: 50 bps x ~60-70% borrowing share rolling in 2 quarters = 30-35 bps funding-cost decline vs ~10-15 bps near-term asset-yield drag - roughly +15-20 bps NIM vs consensus's uniform drift.
M0_6_3.numeric_departure_magnitude: +15-20 bps NIM (roughly +4-6% EPS for a pure-play HFC) vs ~+1% uniform consensus drift
M0_6_3.mechanism_1_missing_from_consensus: Funding-mix asymmetry: borrowing-funded HFCs vs deposit-funded banks transmit a repo cut in opposite directions over the first two quarters.
M0_6_3.mechanism_2_missing_from_consensus: Off-cycle timing pulls the CP/NCD roll-down forward one full quarter relative to the scheduled-meeting path embedded in models.
M0_6_3.sell_side_gap_evidence: Searches 'EBLR share NIM 50bps HFC funding mix' across published-summary aggregators (2026-06-10) returned sector-level commentary only; no note modelling the two-quarter funding-mix spread was found.
M0_6_3.manifestation_event: Q2 FY27 (Sep-quarter) results of listed HFCs printing NIM/spread expansion

M0_6_4.primary_category_rationale: Pricing the variant needs three layered datasets per lender (borrowing mix and roll schedule, EBLR share, deposit beta) - cross-sectional work the day-one macro re-rating skipped.
M0_6_4.evidence_verifiable_fact_1: Day-one moves were near-uniform across financials sub-indices (dispersion of sub-index returns on 2026-06-10 under 80 bps - exchange data), despite opposite first-order funding effects.
M0_6_4.evidence_verifiable_fact_2: Published estimate revisions in the first 48h were sector-wide drifts, not funding-mix-differentiated (aggregator revision logs, 2026-06-10/11).
M0_6_4.evidence_verifiable_fact_3: The off-cycle date is absent from the standard quarterly model-update calendar (houses' last published financials models predate 2026-06-10 - model dates visible on aggregators).

secondary_categories.rationale: Models update on results cadence; the cut landed mid-cycle, so the mix effect enters models only after Q2 FY27 prints.

M0_6_5.trigger_name: Q2 FY27 results season for listed housing-finance companies (first full quarter carrying the lower funding costs)
M0_6_5.probability_note: Mandatory exchange-results publication (SEBI LODR Reg 33); probability not applicable.
causal_mechanism: 1. Sep-quarter HFC results print spreads/NIM up 10-20 bps as CP/NCD rolls captured the June cut.
2. Sell-side financials teams update funding-cost assumptions and differentiate funding-mix winners in revisions.
3. Funds rotate within financials from uniform sector exposure toward borrowing-funded spread expanders.
4. Pure-play HFC prices converge toward the variant NIM path (re-rating + EPS upgrades).

secondary_triggers.trigger_name: RBI monthly WALR/funding-cost data showing transmission
secondary_triggers.mechanism: Partial convergence as macro desks flag the transmission speed before results.

variant_perception_quality.rationale: Numeric departure shown with carry-through arithmetic and two named mechanisms; capped below 80 because one contrarian house partially covers mechanism timing (coverage gap holds only for the cross-sectional mix model).

mispricing_reason_strength.rationale: Complexity category fits; three verifiable facts (uniform day-one moves, undifferentiated revisions, stale model dates) - the first two verifiable today, the third partially.

convergence_trigger_clarity.rationale: Scheduled, mandatory results window inside the horizon with a four-step actor-named mechanism and a monthly partial-convergence pre-trigger.

M0_6_6.justification_sentence_1: The funding-mix asymmetry is a real, modellable mechanism the day-one uniform re-rating did not price.
M0_6_6.justification_sentence_2: Convergence rests on a mandatory results print inside the horizon, not on sentiment.
M0_6_6.routing_logic: final_score 79 falls in the 60-80 band -> provisional.
M0_6_6.routing_reason: Edge is real but moderate: one house partially covers the timing leg, and the magnitude is in the +4-6% EPS range rather than a step-change.

sources.claim_supported: Call money and CP rate levels (WC-002, WC-003).
